food_name: Quaker Instant Oats (plain, 1 sachet, 28g dry, prepared as porridge)

local_names.romanized_local: Guì Gé Jí Shí Yàn Mài

aliases: 桂格即食燕麦 (Guì Gé Jí Shí Yàn Mài)
Guì Gé Jí Shí Yàn Mài

chinese: 桂格即食燕麦 (Guì Gé Jí Shí Yàn Mài)
romanized: Guì Gé Jí Shí Yàn Mài

region_of_origin: Brand: USA (Quaker Oats Company / PepsiCo); product manufactured for Singapore market (Malaysia/Australia)
food_subgroup: Instant oatmeal — pre-cooked, rolled, and dried oat flakes; 1 sachet = 28g dry, prepared with 180ml hot water to yield approximately 200g porridge

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn covers rolled oats generally (FOODON_03301610) but does not have a term specific to the Quaker Instant brand formulation or the instant/quick-cook processing variant. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Oat grain milled products — rolled/flaked oats
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers oat grain milled products including rolled/flaked oats. No brand-specific or instant-oat-specific FoodEx2 term exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for instant oat porridge or Quaker Instant Oats as of 2026. SNOMED CT food coding is limited for processed cereal products.

preparation_method: prepared by adding boiling water (180ml per 28g sachet)

serving.description: 1 sachet = 28g dry oats, prepared with 180ml hot water
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one sachet (28g dry oats) prepared by adding approximately 180ml of boiling water, yielding approximately 200g of porridge.

nutrient_source.title: Quaker Oats Instant Plain — Nutrition Information Panel (Singapore product label) and USDA FoodData Central: Oats, instant, plain, dry (FDC ID 173904)
nutrient_source.organization: Quaker Oats Company / PepsiCo Singapore (primary label data); USDA Agricultural Research Service (secondary cross-reference for micronutrients)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre) sourced from the Quaker Singapore product NIP (Nutrition Information Panel) for the plain instant sachet. Micronutrients (potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, vitamins) cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 173904 (Oats, instant, plain, dry) which is consistent with the Singapore label values. All values are reported per 100g dry weight.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Instant Oats / Quick Oats — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (Glycemic Index Research Service, Sydney)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 58 (medium) is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for instant oats / quick oats category. This is the closest approved GI source match for Quaker Instant Oats plain sachet. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (58 × 18.9g) / 100 ≈ 11.0. Carbohydrate per 28g serving = 67.5 × 0.28 = 18.9g. Confidence 0.9 — direct category match, not brand-specific measurement.

hypertension.key_factor: Zero sodium; potassium 362mg/100g (101mg per 28g serving); beta-glucan soluble fibre supports LDL reduction and cardiovascular risk
hypertension.guidance: The plain Quaker Instant Oats sachet contains zero sodium and provides 101mg potassium per serving, making it one of the most hypertension-friendly breakfast options available; beta-glucan fibre (~1g per sachet) further supports cardiovascular risk reduction by lowering LDL cholesterol, and magnesium (39mg per serving) contributes to vasodilation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI 58 (medium), GL 11 per sachet; beta-glucan viscous soluble fibre slows gastric emptying and reduces postprandial glucose peak
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of 58 and glycemic load of approximately 11 per sachet, Quaker Instant Oats plain is appropriate for Type 2 Diabetes management; the beta-glucan fibre (~1g per 28g sachet) forms a viscous gel in the small intestine that slows glucose absorption and reduces postprandial glucose excursions, and regular consumption supports improved insulin sensitivity — however, patients should be counselled to avoid preparing with Milo, condensed milk, or added sugar, which substantially increase the glycemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Beta-glucan soluble fibre reduces LDL cholesterol; zero sodium; low saturated fat (1.3g/100g); zero trans fat; zero cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Quaker Instant Oats plain is cardioprotective: beta-glucan fibre has an FDA-approved and EFSA-approved health claim for LDL cholesterol reduction (3g beta-glucan per day required; one sachet provides ~1g), saturated fat is low at 1.3g/100g, trans fat and cholesterol are zero, and zero sodium eliminates dietary sodium contribution from this meal — making it a clinically recommended breakfast for cardiovascular disease risk reduction.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Low GI reduces insulin-driven inflammation; beta-glucan supports gut microbiome diversity; magnesium supports anti-inflammatory enzyme pathways
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The low glycemic load and beta-glucan content of plain instant oats support anti-inflammatory dietary patterns relevant to rheumatoid arthritis management, with magnesium (39mg per serving, approximately 10% RDI) contributing to anti-inflammatory enzyme function.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Quaker Instant Oats (plain sachet) is a commercially packaged instant oatmeal product sold widely in Singapore supermarkets and convenience stores. Each sachet contains 28g of pre-cooked, rolled, and dried whole grain oat flakes that are prepared by adding approximately 180ml of boiling water, yielding a smooth, creamy porridge of approximately 200g. The texture is soft and slightly viscous due to the beta-glucan soluble fibre content; the flavour is mild, nutty, and unsweetened in the plain variant. Nutritionally, the dry oats provide 368 kcal/100g, 12.8g protein, 7.1g fat (1.3g saturated), 67.5g carbohydrate, 1.4g sugar, and 10g dietary fibre per 100g. Per 28g serving: approximately 103 kcal, 3.6g protein, 2.0g fat, 18.9g carbohydrate, and 2.8g dietary fibre. The defining clinical advantage is beta-glucan fibre — a viscous soluble fibre unique to oats that forms a gel in the small intestine, physically impeding glucose absorption, reducing LDL cholesterol via bile acid sequestration, and feeding beneficial gut bacteria (Lactobacillus, Bifidobacterium). The GI is approximately 58 (medium), substantially lower than corn flakes (GI 81) or rice-based cereals (GI 72–78), with a glycemic load of approximately 11 per sachet. The plain sachet contains zero sodium and zero added sugar, making it suitable for hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease management. The FDA and EFSA have approved a health claim for oat beta-glucan and LDL cholesterol reduction (3g/day required; one sachet provides ~1g). In Singapore, the product is commonly prepared with hot Milo or condensed milk — both of which significantly increase sugar and caloric load and undermine the glycemic benefit; clinical counselling should address these preparation habits. The product is vegetarian, vegan, halal, and dairy-free. It is the dominant oat brand in Singapore, manufactured regionally (Malaysia/Australia) for the local market by PepsiCo under the Quaker brand.

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from the Quaker Singapore product Nutrition Information Panel (exact match). Micronutrients cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 173904 (Oats, instant, plain, dry) with consistent values. GI sourced from University of Sydney GI Database for instant oats category (confidence 0.9, analogous category match). All values are internally consistent and supported by approved primary sources.
notes: Monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported on the Quaker Singapore product NIP and not available as a brand-specific entry in USDA Branded Foods; USDA SR Legacy FDC 173904 reports approximate values but these are for generic instant oats and not confirmed for this specific Singapore product formulation, so -1 is used to avoid false precision. Water_g set to -1: moisture content of the dry oat product is not reported on the Singapore NIP and varies with preparation method; not applicable to the dry per-100g basis without confirmed source data. Trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per the Quaker Singapore product NIP. Cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero — oats are a plant food and contain no cholesterol (naturally zero). Vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_d_mcg, and vitamin_b12_mcg are confirmed zero — plain instant oats are not fortified with these vitamins in the Singapore plain sachet formulation (confirmed zero per product NIP and consistent with USDA FDC 173904). Sodium_mg is confirmed zero per the Quaker Singapore plain sachet NIP (naturally zero for plain unflavoured oats with no added salt). GL per serving calculated as (58 × 18.9) / 100 = 10.96 ≈ 11.0, where 18.9g carbohydrate = 67.5g/100g × 28g serving. FoodEx2 code A00LC used as closest parent for oat grain milled products; no brand-specific or instant-oat-specific FoodEx2 term confirmed. SNOMED CT code not available for this food type as of 2026.
